title: Xtra Fluffy British Praline Pudding
description: A traditional British dessert with a French twist, featuring praline and tempered chocolate, this dish is perfect for cozy nights in Yorkshire
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, sugar, and praline paste, and heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has dissolved.
In a small bowl, whisk together the flour and melted butter.
Temper the chocolate chips by melting them in a double boiler, then cooling them slightly.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and vanilla extract.
Add the flour mixture to the egg mixture, and whisk until smooth.
Add the milk mixture to the egg mixture, and whisk until well combined.
Pour the mixture into 6 individual serving cups.
Place the serving cups in a large baking dish, and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the cups.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the puddings from the water bath, and let them cool to room temperature.
